extraction.one_line_summary: KÄMNA Lennard won a reduced sprint after Quintana crashed on the penultimate climb requiring bike change, Dumoulin waited then controlled finish.
tactical_patterns: Dumoulin refused to exploit Quintana's mechanical/crash on climb, waited demonstrating tactical confidence in GC control through neutralization over attacking.
Jungels repeatedly attacked late solo despite Luxembourger numbers disadvantage, forcing disorganized chase that favored opportunistic late sprinters like Kämna.
Pozzovivo and Visconti attempted Italian alliance off front but failed to commit cooperatively, allowing stronger finishers to bridge and outsprint.
Quick-Step sixth stage win reveals team depth exploitation: different riders winning different stage profiles throughout three-week race.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: demonstrated psychological dominance by refusing to attack crashed rival, confident enough in GC gap to wait rather than exploit mechanicals
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: crash and bike change on penultimate climb cost momentum but still salvaged second and six-second bonus, showing resilience despite deficit
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: positive: repeatedly attacked solo late despite isolation, forcing stronger GC riders to chase, punching above climbing weight class
rider_signals.MOLLEMA Bauke: negative: attacked post-GC disaster from previous day but lacked sprint speed to finish, revealing desperation rather than form recovery
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: sat on attacks then outsprinted climbers including Quintana and Yates, unexpected finishing speed for reduced group finish
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: neutral: strong enough to make late selection but couldn't commit to cooperation or outsprint, tactically indecisive at critical moment
